rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to Contra Costa County** MARIANA GREUB ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With The Courts October 9, 2024 Minute Order, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2025, line 3, 3 - PLAINTIFFS MARIANA GREUB, ESTON GREUB'S Motion To Compel Compliance With The Courts October 9, 2024 Minute Order, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Plaintiff Mariana Greub's motion to compel compliance with the court's October 9, 2024 order and for sanctions is granted. This court ordered production of documents responsive to RFPs 24-26, 37, 38, 44, 46 as discussed further in the September 13, 2024 judge pro tem recommendation and the court's order of October 9, 2024 adopting the recommendation. The Tran declaration establishes that production has not been made. The court orders defendant American Honda Motor Co. Inc. to produce responsive records within 10 days of entry of this order and to pay sanctions of $1600 to Greub within 30 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

This court ordered further responses within 10 days of its order. Responses have not yet been made. The court orders defendant American Honda Motor Co. Inc. to provide code-compliant responses to the Greub's Special Interrogatories, Set Two, within 10 days of entry of this order, and to pay sanctions of $1600 to Greub within 30 days of entry of this order. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: ANNA JORAYEVA VS. PRESIDIO SURGERY CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2025, line 1, DEFENDANTS PRESIDIO SURGERY CENTER, LLC, SURGICAL CARE AFFILIATES, LLC, TRACY PERELMUTER, ANDRE SCHOTTE'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the IIED claim is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to defendant Schotte. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person.
